economic_notes: Nation owns Kyah Development Corporation (100%), Kyah Food and Fuel Inc. (100%), and Kyah Development Limited Partnership (99.99%). Distributions of $5,000,000 received from Nation business enterprises. Portfolio investments include GICs of $15,367,688 and 10% limited partnership interest in Houston Pellet Limited Partnership.
notes: Audit is qualified for three reasons: (1) no budget was prepared for the year as required by PSAS; (2) financial statements not available for Nation business enterprises accounted for by modified equity method (Kyah Development LP, Kyah Development Corp, Kyah Food and Fuel Inc.); (3) financial statements not available for consolidated business enterprises dependent on the First Nation. Witset Social Housing is the only consolidated subsidiary. TCA net book value not determinable from the text provided as Schedule 1 was referenced but full data not included in the extract. Long-term debt figure is a partial sum as the loan schedule was cut off in the source text. Deferred revenue of $3,739,680 is entirely new in 2023 (was $0 in 2022), driven largely by a $3,450,838 ISC Youth Healing Lodge contribution.
